policy.forbiddenRule: A future M60 promotion/support consumer must not use FOR-293 red_draw_runtime_domain, clip_coverage, or difference_clip_coverage evidence unless it references both FOR-301 and FOR-302 supersession evidence.
policy.guardScope: audit-only; normal renderer, SkAAClip.op, CTM, blend, setPixel, GPU/WebGPU, thresholds, fallbacks, and M60 support status are unchanged.

historicalActions.reason: Predates FOR-301/FOR-302 runtime reconciliation and cannot reopen M60 alone.
Predates FOR-301/FOR-302 runtime reconciliation and cannot reopen M60 alone.

requiredFutureReopenProof: explicit citation of FOR-301 runtime SkAAClip trace
explicit citation of FOR-302 analytic/runtime reconciliation
local pixel-level prediction that differs from the reconciled runtime model
reference, CPU, GPU, diff, stats, and route diagnostics
preserved fallback policy for non-selected rows
no use of FOR-293 as standalone oracle

policyCases.name: FOR-309 gate keeps M60 closed without new hypothesis
policyCases.reason: No post-supersession renderer hypothesis is available, so the gate keeps M60 closed.

policyCases.name: FOR-283/FOR-286 action alone is forbidden
policyCases.reason: Pre-supersession nextAction evidence cannot reopen M60 without FOR-301/FOR-302 and a new hypothesis.

policyCases.name: Post-supersession hypothesis without FOR-301/FOR-302 is ambiguous
policyCases.reason: A post-supersession hypothesis must cite FOR-301 and FOR-302.

policyCases.name: Support claim without complete proof is forbidden
policyCases.reason: M60 support requires complete rendered evidence.

policyCases.name: Cited hypothesis can become future audit candidate
policyCases.reason: A cited post-supersession hypothesis may open a future audit, but not a support claim.
